codeReview.summary: The commit adds a locale module and some Preferences tab-label plumbing, but it does not actually land the persisted language setting promised by the feature and it does not wire the visible selector into the live Preferences viewport. As a result, the feature does not satisfy the required English-default or visible-selector behavior.

issues.description: This commit never adds the persisted language foundation it claims to introduce. `Settings` still has no `ui_language` field and the file contains no `Language` enum, yet `rust/src/locale.rs:6` imports `crate::settings::Language` and `rust/src/native_ui/preferences.rs:372`/`:381`/`:386` read `self.settings.ui_language`. The reviewed commit therefore cannot provide the promised backward-compatible persisted language setting and does not stand on its own.

The active Preferences window still does not expose the new selector or an English-first UI. `show()` builds the viewport with the hard-coded Chinese title `"CodexBar 设置"` and renders through `render_settings_ui()`/`render_general_tab()` (lines 340 and 3344), while the only language selector lives in the legacy `show_general_tab()` helper at lines 358-392, which is never called. The live renderer also keeps hard-coded Chinese control text in `render_general_tab()`, so the feature misses both 'visible English/Chinese selector in Preferences' and 'clean first launch shows English app-owned labels by default'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The active Preferences surface is the viewport renderer path (`show()` -> `render_settings_ui()` -> `render_*`), while `show_*` helpers are legacy dead code kept only for potential future use. That distinction is not called out in mission shared state and was easy for the worker to miss.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The reviewed file itself marks legacy helpers with `#![allow(dead_code)] // Legacy show_* methods kept for potential future use` at `rust/src/native_ui/preferences.rs:5`. The live path is `show()` -> `render_settings_ui()` at lines 267-340, while the worker transcript spends time inspecting and relying on the selector inside `show_general_tab()` at lines 358-392.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `ui-i18n-worker` skill expects manual Wine/Xvfb verification of the exact UI surface, but this worker ended the feature without that proof. Stronger skill guidance or validation around required interactive evidence would help prevent false-positive completions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/fsos/Developer/Win-CodexBar/.factory/skills/ui-i18n-worker/SKILL.md` step 6 requires manual Wine/Xvfb verification. The handoff for session `f3a83c18-dbc5-4d80-9b7d-22be115bfff4` records `interactiveChecks: []` and only logs `xvfb-run -a wine codexbar.exe menubar --help`; the transcript also states that manual verification was 'challenging' and proceeds to EndFeatureRun anyway.

summary: Review failed. The commit adds locale scaffolding, but it neither introduces the persisted `ui_language` setting nor wires the live Preferences viewport to show the language selector and English-default labels.
